subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Heat Index and Danger Zones
description: A 60-second lesson on Heat Index and Danger Zones.

hook.text: It's 90°F with 80% humidity. The heat index says it 'feels like' 113°F. That number can kill.

buildup.text: The heat index combines temperature and humidity to show how hot your body actually experiences.

discovery.text: Above 130°F heat index, heatstroke is highly likely with any physical activity.

twist.text: The heat index is calculated for shade. In direct sunlight, add up to 15°F to the value.

climax.text: Weather services issue heat advisories based on heat index, not just air temperature.

punchline.text: The thermometer lies in humidity. The heat index tells your body's truth.

quiz.question: Why is the heat index more useful than temperature alone?
options: It accounts for humidity's effect on the body's ability to cool
